---
title: "Labor and Execution OS"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "talos"
  - "talos_md"
  - "viea"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "software_magic_grimoire"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "moecot"
  - "spinoza_composer"
  - "ladon_manhattan"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Labor and Execution OS

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `talos`, `talos_md`, `viea`, `genesiscode`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `spinoza_composer`, `ladon_manhattan` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from the handoff outline and source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Reasoning must become typed work with artifacts, permissions, audit logs, and governed side effects.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

A generic agent loop does not provide typed job lifecycle, replay, artifact graph discipline, or human approval gates.

## Core claim

[08-core, support: argument] The execution layer should convert plans into typed jobs and artifacts through a governed labor operating system.

## Mechanism

- Compile command contracts into jobs.
- Run jobs through adapters with permissions and audit logs.
- Promote repeated workflows into verified tools when evidence supports it.

## Interfaces

- Planning emits job contracts.
- Governance enforces authority and approvals.
- Evidence layers reconstruct audit trails and replay results.

## Invariants

- Side effects pass through explicit authorization.
- Artifacts are addressable and auditable.
- Human approval gates remain enforceable.

## Failure modes

- Tool misuse.
- Unlogged side effects.
- Workflow-to-tool promotion without evidence.

## Minimal implementation

A typed job schema, artifact graph, permission model, audit log, and replay protocol.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Typed job lifecycle test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Tool permission enforcement test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Audit log reconstruction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Replay determinism test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Human approval gate test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Adversarial job injection test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/handoff: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `talos_md` | Talos_Protocol_v1.0.md | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Markdown/public release version. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `software_magic_grimoire` | Software Magic Grimoire | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Structured command contracts and semantic density of software words. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `spinoza_composer` | Spinoza Composer / Spinoza Trinity | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Narrative/compliance operating system variant. Use as applied Spinoza. |
| `ladon_manhattan` | Ladon & The Manhattan Protocol | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Kernel-level security architecture for high-agency AI. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest the assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
